article_items.description: A Blog post by Wortega on Hugging Face

article_items.summary: The mix is what the model sees at training time with noise augmentation enabled. vLLM ships a closed set of multimodal architectures out of the box ( Qwen2-Audio , LLaVA , Phi-4-MM , a couple more). The practical impact: When the user writes a prompt with one <|AUDIO|> , the processor expands it into <|start_of_audio|> + 375× <|AUDIO|> + <|start_of_audio|> , and marks each of those 375 tokens as "embedding will be supplied externally" via PromptUpdateDetails.select_token_id(..., embed_token_id=audio_token_id) . Where the 2.1× comes from. Watch next: The plugin ( vllm_borealis ) sits next to the weights in the HF model repo. Where the 2.1× comes from.

article_items.title: OpenAI, Grupo Folha, and Grupo UOL announce strategic content partnership
article_items.description: OpenAI partners with Grupo Folha and Grupo UOL to bring trusted Brazilian journalism to ChatGPT, expanding access to news with attribution and transparency.
article_items.excerpt: OpenAI partners with Grupo Folha and Grupo UOL to bring trusted Brazilian journalism to ChatGPT, expanding access to news with attribution and transparency.
article_items.summary: OpenAI partners with Grupo Folha and Grupo UOL to bring trusted Brazilian journalism to ChatGPT, expanding access to news with attribution and transparency. By partnering with OpenAI, we are putting Folha at the forefront of this transformation and creating new ways to expand the reach, relevance, and impact of trusted journalism,” says Carlos Ponce de Leon, Folha de S.Paulo's Co-CEO. The practical impact: By partnering with publishers across multiple markets, including in the United States, UK, France, Germany and now Brazil, OpenAI aims to integrate trusted reporting into AI-powered experiences and help bring journalism into ChatGPT in ways that emphasize attribution, transparency, and links back to original sources. It is only natural that they partner with the creators of high-quality content for that,” says Paulo Samia, CEO of UOL.

“AI will define the next era of the news industry and Folha wants to help shape that future.
This is OpenAI’s first media partnership in Brazil and grounded in a broader effort to work collaboratively with news organizations around the world.

article_items.summary: The full text of Chris Olah's remarks on the Pope's encyclical on AI Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah's remarks on Pope Leo XIV's encyclical "Magnifica humanitas" On Monday May 25, 2026, Pope Leo XIV released an encyclical on the topic of AI: "Magnifica humanitas: On safeguarding the human person in the time of artificial Intelligence." Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah was invited to speak at the presentation of the encyclical in the Vatican City, doing so as part of Anthropic’s initiative to widen the... I lead a research team that studies the internal structure of these models - what is actually happening inside... The third is the need for discernment on the nature of AI models.
They are mistaken: the questions raised by AI are bigger than the AI research community, not just in their implications, but also in their nature.

Widening the conversation on frontier AI KPMG integrates Claude across its core business and workforce of more than 276,000 in strategic alliance KPMG and Anthropic announce a global alliance, with Claude integrated...
The pressure to stay commercially viable and to stay at the research frontier.
The first is our duty to the global poor.
The machinery that makes this possible is the work of math and programming and science.
And the older, plainer pressures of pride and ambition.
If AI models are going to be widespread, what does it look like for humans, families, and the world to flourish?
I lead a research team that studies the internal structure of these models - what is actually happening inside them.
The second is the need for moral imagination and ambition regarding human flourishing.
It is an unsolved problem, and it is the kind of problem the Church has historically refused to let the world ignore.

article_items.title: What ClickUp's mass layoff tells us about the future of work | TechCrunch
article_items.description: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
article_items.excerpt: The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
article_items.summary: Last Thursday, Evans announced on X that the company, which was last valued in 2021 at $4 billion , had laid off 22% of its workforce yet characterized that reduction as not a cost-cutting measure, but rather a radical embrace of AI that will propel the company to the next level. ClickUp recently introduced roughly 3,000 internal AI agents to handle a wide range of complex tasks on behalf of its employees, according to a Fortune article published several days ago. The practical impact: Instead of performing the work themselves, staff members are now expected to direct these agents and ultimately review the output to ensure it meets the company’s standards. “Most savings from this change will flow directly back into the people who stay. Watch next: More from TechCrunch What ClickUp’s mass layoff tells us about the future of work AI’s biggest champions have argued for some time that the technology will usher in an era of unprecedented productivity gains, richly rewarding workers who harness it while displacing those who don’t. The first StrictlyVC...

That efficiency is apparently paying off: Polsia just raised $30 million at a $250 million valuation.
Evans told TechCrunch via email that the startup is indeed seeing productivity gains from AI agents.
The nine-year-old startup is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents.
Instead of performing the work themselves, staff members are now expected to direct these agents and ultimately review the output to ensure it meets the company’s standards.

Evans’s goal, according to his X post, is for AI to turbocharge ClickUp into a “100x org.” ClickUp is not alone in its hope that AI agents will provide massive productivity gains.
Zeb Evans, CEO of the collaboration software startup ClickUp, claims that this shift is imminent.
